blocks:
Sūrat Fāṭir

Makkan, and it has 45 verses

(1) الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ فاطِرِ السَّماواتِ وَالْأَرْضِ (All praise belongs to Allah, originator of the heavens and the earth) [He is] their originator (مبدعهما) from al-fiṭr (الفطر), meaning splitting (الشقّ), as if He split non-existence (العدم) by bringing them out of it. جاعِلِ الْمَلائِكَةِ رُسُلاً (who made the angels messengers) intermediaries (وسائط) between Allah and His prophets and the righteous among His servants, conveying His messages to them through revelation (الوحي), inspiration (الإلهام), and the truthful dream (الرّؤيا الصادقة). أُولِي أَجْنِحَةٍ مَثْنى وَثُلاثَ وَرُباعَ (having wings, two, three and four [pairs]) possessors of multiple wings (أجنحة متعدّدة) with which they descend, ascend, and hasten toward what they have been commanded.

In al-Kāfī, narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله: "The angels are of three parts: a part having two wings, a part having three wings, and a part having four wings." It was said that perhaps He did not intend the specificity of these numbers or negate what exceeds them, due to what was narrated from him عليه السلام: that He saw Gabriel (جبرئيل) on the Night of Ascension (ليلة المعراج) having six hundred thousand wings.

I say: Perhaps this is what is alluded to in the Almighty's saying: يَزِيدُ فِي الْخَلْقِ ما يَشاءُ. (He adds to the creation whatever He wishes.)

And in al-Ikmāl, narrated from him عليه السلام: "Indeed, Allah, Blessed and Exalted is He, has an angel called Dardā’īl (دردائيل) who has sixteen thousand wings. The space between one wing and the next is air (هواء), and the air is like the space between the heaven and the earth."

And al-Qummiyy narrated from al-Sādiq عليه السلام, who said: "Allah created the angels differently. The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله saw Gabriel, and he had six hundred wings. On his shin (ساقه) was pearl (الدّر) like drops (القطر) on vegetation (البقل). He filled what is between the heaven and the earth." And He said: "When Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, commands Michael (ميكائيل) to descend to the world (الدنيا), his right leg becomes in the seventh heaven and the other in the seventh earth. And indeed, Allah has angels, half of whom are made of cold (برد) and half of whom are made of fire (نار). They say: 'O You Who unites the cold and the fire, keep our hearts firm upon Your obedience.'" And He said: "Indeed, Allah has an angel whose distance between the lobe of his ear (شحمة أذنه) and his eye is a journey of five hundred years by the flapping (بخفقان) of a bird." And He said: "Indeed, the angels do not eat, nor drink, nor marry (ينكحون); rather, they live by the breeze (نسيم) of the Throne (العرش). And indeed, Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, has angels bowing (ركّعاً) until the Day of Resurrection. And indeed, Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, has angels prostrating (سُجَّداً) until the Day of Resurrection." Then Abū ‘Abd Allāh عليه السلام said: The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله said: "There is nothing among what Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, created that is more numerous than the angels. And indeed, seventy thousand angels descend every day or every night. They come to the Sacred House (البيت الحرام) and circumambulate it (يطوفون به). Then they come to the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله. Then they come to the Commander of the Faithful (أمير المؤمنين) عليه السلام and greet him. Then they come to al-Husayn عليه السلام and remain with him. When it is dawn (السحر), an ascension ladder (معراج) is placed for them to the heaven, and they never return."

And Abū Ja‘far عليه السلام said: "Indeed, Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, created Isrāfīl (إسرافيل), Gabriel (جبرائيل), and Michael (ميكائيل) from a single glorification (تسبيحة واحدة), and He granted them hearing (السمع), sight (البصر), excellence of intellect (جودة العقل), and swiftness of understanding (سرعة الفهم)." And the Commander of the Faithful عليه السلام said regarding the creation of the angels: "And angels whom You created and settled in Your heavens: there is no slackness (فترة) in them, nor heedlessness (غفلة) with them, nor disobedience (معصية) in them. They are the most knowledgeable of Your creation concerning You, the most fearful of Your creation toward You, the closest of Your creation to You, and the most active of them in Your obedience. Sleep of the eyes does not cover them, nor forgetfulness (سهو) of the minds, nor weariness (فترة) of the bodies. They did not dwell in loins (الأصلاب), nor were they enclosed by wombs (الأرحام), nor did You create them from despicable water (ماء مهين). You originated them by creation (إنشاء), so You settled them in Your heavens, honored them by Your proximity (جوارك), entrusted them with Your revelation (وحيك), kept them away from afflictions (الآفات), protected them from calamities (البليّات), and purified them from sins (الذنوب). Were it not for Your strength, they would not be strong; were it not for Your firmness, they would not be firm; were it not for Your mercy, they would not obey; and were it not for You, they would not exist. Yet, despite their standing with You, their obedience to You, their rank with You, and their lack of heedlessness concerning Your command, if they were to witness what is hidden from them concerning You, they would belittle their deeds (لاحتقروا أعمالهم), and they would blame (لَأَزرؤا) themselves, and they would know that they have not worshipped You with the worship that is rightfully due to You. Glory be to You, O Creator and O Worshipped One! How excellent is Your favor (بلاءك) upon Your creation!"

And in al-Tawhīd, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful عليه السلام: He was asked about the power (قدرة) of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, so He stood up delivering a sermon (خطيباً). He praised Allah and lauded Him, then He said: "Indeed, Allah, Blessed and Exalted is He, has angels, such that if one of them were to descend to the earth, it would not contain him due to the greatness of his creation and the multitude of his wings. And among them are those whom, if the jinn (الجنّ) and mankind (الإنس) were tasked to describe, they would not be able to describe him due to the distance between his joints (مفاصله) and the beauty of the composition of his form (صورته). And how can one describe those of His angels for whom the distance between his shoulder (منكبه) and the lobe of his ear (شحمة أذنيه) is seven hundred years? And among them are those who block the horizon (الأفق) with one of his wings, without [considering] the greatness of his body. And among them are those for whom the heavens reach up to his waist (حجزته). And among them are those whose foot rests without a fixed place (غير قرار) in the lower atmosphere (جوّ الهوى الأسفل), and the earths reach up to his knees. And among them are those such that if all the waters (جميع المياه) were poured into the hollow of his thumb (نقرة إبهامه), it would contain them. And among them are those such that if a ship (السّفينة) were cast upon the tears of his eyes, it would sail for eternity (دهر الداهرين). So Blessed is Allah, the Best of creators." And in al-Kāfī, narrated from al-Thumālī, who said: "I entered upon ‘Alī ibn al-Husayn عليهما السلام and waited in the house for an hour. Then I entered the room, and He was picking up something and putting His hand from behind the curtain, handing it to whoever was in the room. I said: 'May I be made your ransom (جعلت فداك), what is this thing I see You picking up?' He said: 'It is a remnant (فضلة) of the down (زغب) of the angels. We collect it when we are alone and make it into prayer beads (سبحاً) for our children.' I said: 'May I be made your ransom, do they really come to you?' He said: 'O Abā Ḥamzah, they indeed crowd us upon our cushions (تكأتنا).'" And there are many narrations with this meaning in it [i.e., al-Kāfī] and in al-Baṣā’ir: يَزِيدُ فِي الْخَلْقِ ما يَشاءُ (He adds to the creation whatever He wishes) according to the requirement of His wisdom (حكمته).

In al-Tawhīd, narrated from al-Sādiq عليه السلام: "Indeed, al-Qaḍā’ (القضاء) and al-Qadar (القدر) are two creations among the creations of Allah, and Allah يَزِيدُ فِي الْخَلْقِ ما يَشاءُ (adds to the creation whatever He wishes)." And in al-Majma‘, narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله: "It is the beautiful face (الوجه الحسن), the beautiful voice (الصوت الحسن), and the beautiful hair (الشعر الحسن)." إِنَّ اللهَ عَلى كُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدِيرٌ (Indeed Allah has power over all things.)

(1) Khāfaqa al-ṭā’ir (خفق الطائر) means the bird flew (طار), and akhfaqa (اخفق) means when it struck with its wings (بجناحيه).

(٢) ما يَفْتَحِ اللهُ لِلنَّاسِ Whatever He releases for them مِنْ رَحْمَةٍ such as a blessing (نعمة), security (أمن), health (صحّة), knowledge (علم), prophethood (نبوّة), and guardianship (ولاية). And al-Qummi narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) that He said: And al-Mut'ah (المتعة) is part of that. فَلا مُمْسِكَ لَها who can restrain it وَما يُمْسِكْ فَلا مُرْسِلَ لَهُ who can release it مِنْ بَعْدِهِ after His withholding it وَهُوَ الْعَزِيزُ the Dominant over whatever He wills, and there is none who can dispute with Him concerning it الْحَكِيمُ He does not act except with knowledge and perfection (إتقان).

(2) Whatever Allah opens up for the people of His mercy, there is none who can withhold it; and whatever He withholds, there is none who can release it after Him. He is the All-mighty, the All-wise.

(٣) يا أَيُّهَا النَّاسُ اذْكُرُوا نِعْمَتَ اللهِ عَلَيْكُمْ ** (O mankind, remember Allah’s blessing upon you) Preserve it by knowing its due right, acknowledging it, and obeying its Bestower. هَلْ مِنْ خالِقٍ غَيْرُ اللهِ يَرْزُقُكُمْ مِنَ السَّماءِ وَالْأَرْضِ لا إِلهَ إِلَّا هُوَ فَأَنَّى تُؤْفَكُونَ ** (Is there any creator other than Allah who provides for you from the sky and the earth? There is no god except Him. So where are you being led away?) So from which aspect are you being turned away from Monotheism (التوحيد) to associating others with Him? And it was read (وقرئ) with (غير) not being in the genitive case (مجروراً).

(4) ** وَإِنْ يُكَذِّبُوكَ فَقَدْ كُذِّبَتْ رُسُلٌ مِنْ قَبْلِكَ ** (4) If they deny you, apostles before you have been denied. meaning, take them as an example (فتأسّ بهم) in patience regarding their denial. وَإِلَى اللهِ تُرْجَعُ الْأُمُورُ (and to Allah all matters are returned) so that He may reward you and them for the patience and the denial (respectively).

(5) ** يا أَيُّهَا النَّاسُ إِنَّ وَعْدَ اللهِ ** (5) O mankind! Allah’s promise concerning the Gathering (الحشر) and the recompense (الجزاء) حَقٌ ** (is true) there is no deviation (خلف) in it. فَلا تَغُرَّنَّكُمُ الْحَياةُ الدُّنْيا ** (So do not let the life of the world deceive you) lest enjoying it distract you (فيذهلكم) from seeking the Hereafter and striving for it. وَلا يَغُرَّنَّكُمْ بِاللهِ الْغَرُورُ ** (and do not let the Deceiver deceive you concerning Allah.) The Deceiver (الْغَرُورُ) is Satan (الشيطان), by promising you forgiveness while you persist in disobedience (المعصية).

(٦) ** إِنَّ الشَّيْطانَ لَكُمْ عَدُوٌّ ** (6) (Indeed, Satan is your enemy) — a general, ancient enmity. ** فَاتَّخِذُوهُ عَدُوًّا ** (so take him for an enemy) — in your beliefs (عقائدكم) and your actions (أفعالكم), and be wary of him in all aspects of your conditions. ** إِنَّما يَدْعُوا حِزْبَهُ لِيَكُونُوا مِنْ أَصْحابِ السَّعِيرِ ** (He only invites his faction that they may be among the inmates of the Blaze.) — This is an affirmation of his enmity and a clarification of his purpose.

(٧) الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا لَهُمْ عَذابٌ شَدِيدٌ وَالَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَعَمِلُوا الصَّالِحاتِ لَهُمْ مَغْفِرَةٌ وَأَجْرٌ كَبِيرٌ ** (7) For those who are faithless there is a severe punishment, and for those who have faith and do righteous deeds there is forgiveness and a great reward. A threat (وعيد) for him who answered His call (دعاءه) and a promise (وعد) for him who opposed Him. (٨) أَفَمَنْ زُيِّنَ لَهُ سُوءُ عَمَلِهِ فَرَآهُ حَسَناً ** (8) Is he whose evil deed is made fair-seeming to him, and he regards it as good, [like one who is rightly guided]? [Is he] like one for whom it was not made fair-seeming, but rather he was granted success (وُفِّق) until he recognized the truth? The answer [to the conditional question] was omitted because what follows indicates it: فَإِنَّ اللهَ يُضِلُّ مَنْ يَشاءُ وَيَهْدِي مَنْ يَشاءُ. For Allah leads astray whomever He wishes, and guides whomever He wishes.

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Kazim (peace be upon him): He was asked about the self-admiration (العجب) which corrupts deeds. He said: "Self-admiration has degrees (درجات), among them is that the servant's evil deed is made fair-seeming to him, so he regards it as good, and it pleases him, and he thinks that he is doing good work (يُحْسِنُ صُنْعاً)."

فَلا تَذْهَبْ نَفْسُكَ عَلَيْهِمْ حَسَراتٍ ** So do not let your soul be wasted in regrets for their sake. So do not destroy your soul over them due to regrets (الحسرات) over their error (غَيِّهِمْ) and their persistence (إِصْرَارِهِمْ) in denial (التكذيب). إِنَّ اللهَ عَلِيمٌ بِما يَصْنَعُونَ ** Indeed, Allah is knowing of what they do. So He will recompense them for it.

Al-Qummi, narrated in a raised chain (مرفوعاً), said: It was revealed concerning Zurayq and Habtar.

(٩) وَاللهُ الَّذِي أَرْسَلَ الرِّياحَ ** (9) It is Allah who sends the winds And it was read (وقرئ) الرّيح (the wind) فَتُثِيرُ سَحاباً فَسُقْناهُ إِلى بَلَدٍ مَيِّتٍ فَأَحْيَيْنا بِهِ الْأَرْضَ (and they raise a cloud, then We drive it to a dead land, and revive the earth with it) with the rain descending from it بَعْدَ مَوْتِها ** (after its death) after its dryness (يبسها).

In al-Kafi and al-Qummi, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): He was asked about the cloud (السّحاب), where it resides. He said: "It resides upon trees, upon a sand dune (كثيب), upon the seashore, taking refuge there. Then, when Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, wills to send it, أَرْسَلَ ** (He sends) a wind, which stirs it up. Then He entrusts angels to it, who strike it with scourges (المخاريق), and that is the lightning (البرق), so it rises."

And it was added in al-Kafi: Then he recited this verse: وَاللهُ الَّذِي أَرْسَلَ الرِّياحَ ** (It is Allah who sends the winds) [the rest of] the verse. He said: "And the angel's name is the Thunder (الرّعد)." كَذلِكَ النُّشُورُ (Thus will be the resurrection) meaning: the revival of the dead lands is like the revival of the dead people.

And it has preceded from the interpretation of the Imam (peace be upon him) in the story of the Cow (قصّة البقرة): that Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, sends down, between the two blasts of the Trumpet (الصّور), after the first blast is blown, from below the lowest heaven, from the Swollen Sea (الْبَحْرِ الْمَسْجُورِ) which Allah the Almighty mentioned: الْبَحْرِ الْمَسْجُورِ ** (6) by the swollen sea, and it is semen (منّي) like the semen of men. This rain falls upon the earth, and the seminal water (الماء المنيّ) meets the decayed dead bodies, so they sprout from the earth and are revived.

In al-Majalis and al-Qummi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): When Allah wills to resurrect creation, He causes the sky to rain upon the earth for forty mornings, so the joints (الأوصال) are gathered and the flesh sprouts.

(١٠)** مَنْ كانَ يُرِيدُ الْعِزَّةَ فَلِلَّهِ الْعِزَّةُ جَمِيعاً ** (10) Whoever desires might—all might belongs to Allah.

** مَنْ كانَ يُرِيدُ الْعِزَّةَ ** (Whoever desires might) means honor (الشرف) and benefit (المنفعة). **فَلِلَّهِ الْعِزَّةُ جَمِيعاً ** (all might belongs to Allah) meaning, let him seek it from Him, for all of it belongs to Him.

In al-Majma', narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله, he said: "Indeed, your Lord says every day: 'I am the All-mighty (العزيز). So whoever desires the might (عزّ) of the two abodes (الدارين), let him obey the All-mighty (العزيز).'"

** إِلَيْهِ يَصْعَدُ الْكَلِمُ الطَّيِّبُ وَالْعَمَلُ الصَّالِحُ يَرْفَعُهُ ** To Him ascends the good word, and the righteous deed elevates it.

It was said: This is a clarification of that by which might (العزّة) is sought, which is monotheism (التّوحيد) and righteous deed (العمل الصّالح).

And al-Qummi said: The word of sincerity (كلمة الإخلاص) and the affirmation of what Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله brought from Allah—of the obligatory duties (الفرائض) and the guardianship (الولاية)—elevates **الْعَمَلُ الصَّالِحُ ** (the righteous deed) to Allah.

And narrated from al-Sadiq عليه السلام: الْكَلِمُ الطَّيِّبُ ** (the good word) is the saying of the believer: "There is no god but Allah, Muhammad is the Messenger of Allah, Ali is the Guardian (وليّ) of Allah and the Successor (خليفة) of the Messenger of Allah," may the blessings of Allah be upon them both. He said: وَالْعَمَلُ الصَّالِحُ ** (and the righteous deed) is the conviction in the heart that this is the truth from Allah, without doubt, from the Lord of the worlds.

And narrated from al-Baqir عليه السلام, he said: The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله said: "Indeed, every saying has a verification (مصداقاً) from a deed that either confirms it or denies it. So when the son of Adam speaks and his saying is confirmed by his deed, his saying is elevated to Allah by his deed. But when he speaks and his deed contradicts his saying, his saying is rejected upon his wicked deed, and he is cast down into the Fire by it."

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq عليه السلام regarding this Ayah, he said: "It is our guardianship (ولايتنا), the People of the House (اهْل البيت)," and He pointed with His hand to His chest. "So whoever does not take us as guardians (يتولّنا), Allah will not elevate any deed for him."

And in al-Ihtijaj, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful عليه السلام: "Whoever says 'There is no god but Allah' sincerely (مخلصاً), his sins are erased just as the black letter is erased from the white parchment (الرّق الأبيض). And when he says 'There is no god but Allah' sincerely a second time, it pierces the gates of heaven and the ranks of the angels, until the angels say to one another: 'Be humbled before the greatness of Allah's command.' And when he says 'There is no god but Allah' sincerely a third time, it does not stop short of the Throne (العرش). Then the Majestic One (الجليل) says: 'Be silent! By My Might (عزّتي) and My Majesty (جلالي), I shall surely forgive the one who said it, regardless of what he was involved in.'" Then He recited this Ayah: إِلَيْهِ يَصْعَدُ الْكَلِمُ الطَّيِّبُ وَالْعَمَلُ الصَّالِحُ يَرْفَعُهُ (To Him ascends the good word, and the righteous deed elevates it), meaning that if his deed is sincere (خالصاً), his saying and his speech are elevated.

** وَالَّذِينَ يَمْكُرُونَ السَّيِّئاتِ لَهُمْ عَذابٌ شَدِيدٌ وَمَكْرُ أُولئِكَ هُوَ يَبُورُ ** But those who devise evil schemes—there is a severe punishment for them, and their scheming will come to naught.

وَالَّذِينَ يَمْكُرُونَ السَّيِّئاتِ ** (But those who devise evil schemes) means the evil schemes (المكرات السيّئات). It was said: This refers to the schemes (مكرات) of Quraysh against the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله in Dar al-Nadwah (دار النّدوة), and their differing (١) in opinion regarding one of three options: imprisoning him, killing him, or expelling him.

I say: And this includes the schemes (مكرات) of the companions of al-Saqifah (السّقيفة) in rejecting the will (وصيّة) of the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله concerning the Successor (الوصيّ) and other matters. لَهُمْ عَذابٌ شَدِيدٌ (there is a severe punishment for them) which is insignificant compared to what they scheme with. وَمَكْرُ أُولئِكَ هُوَ يَبُورُ ** (and their scheming will come to naught) meaning, it will fail (يفسد) and not be executed (لا ينفذ), and ultimately, it will encompass them.

(١١) وَاللهُ خَلَقَكُمْ مِنْ تُرابٍ ثُمَّ مِنْ نُطْفَةٍ ثُمَّ جَعَلَكُمْ أَزْواجاً ** (11) Allah created you from dust, then from a drop of semen, then He made you pairs, males and females. وَما تَحْمِلُ مِنْ أُنْثى وَلا تَضَعُ إِلَّا بِعِلْمِهِ (11) and no female conceives or delivers except by His knowledge. except that it is known to Him. وَما يُعَمَّرُ مِنْ مُعَمَّرٍ وَلا يُنْقَصُ مِنْ عُمُرِهِ إِلَّا فِي كِتابٍ ** (11) No one who is granted a long life is granted a long life, nor is anything curtailed from his life, but it is in a Book. Al-Qummi said: It means it is written in a Book (كتاب). He said: And this is a refutation against those who deny al-Bada' (البداء).

And in al-Jawami', it is said that its meaning is: No life is prolonged وَلا يُنْقَصُ إِلَّا فِي كِتابٍ ** (nor is anything curtailed from his life, but it is in a Book). And this is that it is written in al-Lawh (اللوح): If so-and-so obeys Allah, he remains until such-and-such a time, and if he disobeys, his lifespan which was appointed for him is curtailed. To this, the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله referred: "Indeed, charity (الصدقة) and maintaining kinship ties (صلة الرحم) populate the homes (الديار) and increase the lifespans (الأعمار)."

And in al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq عليه السلام: "We do not know of anything that increases lifespan except maintaining kinship ties (صلة الرحم), such that a man's appointed term (أجل) might be three years, but he maintains kinship ties, so Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, increases his life by thirty years, making it thirty-three years. And his appointed term might be thirty-three years, but he severs kinship ties (قاطعاً للرحم), so Allah curtails thirty years from his life, making his appointed term three years. And the narrations concerning this meaning are very numerous."

إِنَّ ذلِكَ عَلَى اللهِ يَسِيرٌ ** (11) That is indeed easy for Allah. referring to the preservation (الحفظ), the increase (الزيادة), and the curtailment (النقص).

footnote (١) *يتدارءون الحديث* (They dispute the discourse) means they push it away (*يتدافعونه*), so each one of them repels the saying of his companion with the saying that benefits him.

(١٢) وَما يَسْتَوِي الْبَحْرانِ هذا عَذْبٌ فُراتٌ سائِغٌ شَرابُهُ وَهذا مِلْحٌ أُجاجٌ. (12) The two seas are not alike: this one palatable, sweet, pleasant to drink, and that one salty, bitter. al-Qummi narrates from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): al-Ujaj (الأجاج) is the bitter (المرّ). It is said that this is a similitude for the believer (المؤمن) and the disbeliever (الكافر). وَمِنْ كُلٍّ تَأْكُلُونَ لَحْماً طَرِيًّا وَتَسْتَخْرِجُونَ حِلْيَةً تَلْبَسُونَها ** Yet from each you eat fresh meat, and extract ornaments that you wear— pearls (اللّئالي) and rubies (اليواقيت). وَتَرَى الْفُلْكَ فِيهِ مَواخِرَ (١) [meaning] splitting the water with their movement. al-Qummi says: al-Fulk (الْفُلْكَ) [means the ships] approaching and departing with a single wind, لِتَبْتَغُوا مِنْ فَضْلِهِ —from the grace of Allah through movement (بالنقلة) therein—وَلَعَلَّكُمْ تَشْكُرُونَ ** for that.

(١٣)** يُولِجُ اللَّيْلَ فِي النَّهارِ وَيُولِجُ النَّهارَ فِي اللَّيْلِ وَسَخَّرَ الشَّمْسَ وَالْقَمَرَ كُلٌّ يَجْرِي لِأَجَلٍ مُسَمًّى ذلِكُمُ اللهُ رَبُّكُمْ لَهُ الْمُلْكُ وَالَّذِينَ تَدْعُونَ مِنْ دُونِهِ ما يَمْلِكُونَ مِنْ قِطْمِيرٍ ** (13) He makes the night pass into the day, and makes the day pass into the night, and He has subjected the sun and the moon, each moving to a designated term. That is Allah, your Lord; to Him belongs the sovereignty, and those whom you invoke besides Him do not own so much as the speck on a date-stone.

Al-Qummi said: [It is] the thin skin (الجلدة الرّقيقة) which is on the surface (ظهر) of the date-stone (النّوى).

(١٤) إِنْ تَدْعُوهُمْ لا يَسْمَعُوا دُعاءَكُمْ ** (14) If you invoke them, they will not hear your invocation, because they are inanimate objects (جماد). ** وَلَوْ سَمِعُوا ** and even if they heard, hypothetically (على سبيل الفرض), ** مَا اسْتَجابُوا لَكُمْ ** they would not answer you, due to their lack of ability to do so. ** وَيَوْمَ الْقِيامَةِ يَكْفُرُونَ بِشِرْكِكُمْ وَلا يُنَبِّئُكَ مِثْلُ خَبِيرٍ ** and on the Day of Resurrection they will disavow your polytheism. No one will inform you like an All-aware. And no informant will inform you of the matter like an All-aware (مِثْلُ خَبِيرٍ) who has informed you of it, and that is Allah, glory be to Him, for He is the All-aware (الخبير) of it in reality, unlike all other informants. The intent is to confirm what He has informed concerning the state of their deities and to negate what they claim for them.

(١٥) يا أَيُّهَا النَّاسُ أَنْتُمُ الْفُقَراءُ إِلَى اللهِ ** (15) O mankind! You are the poor needing Allah, in your souls and your states. وَاللهُ هُوَ الْغَنِيُّ الْحَمِيدُ (and Allah is the All-sufficient, the All-laudable), the One Who is absolutely independent (المستغني على الإطلاق), the Bestower of favors upon all existing things (سائر الموجودات), such that He has deserved praise from them. (١٦) إِنْ يَشَأْ يُذْهِبْكُمْ وَيَأْتِ بِخَلْقٍ جَدِيدٍ ** (16) If He wishes, He can take you away and bring a new creation. with other people who are more obedient than you. (١٧) وَما ذلِكَ عَلَى اللهِ بِعَزِيزٍ ** (17) And that is not difficult for Allah. by being impossible (بمتعذّر) or difficult (متعسّر). (١٨) وَلا تَزِرُ وازِرَةٌ وِزْرَ أُخْرى ** (18) No bearer shall bear another’s burden, and no sinful soul shall bear the sin of another soul. As for His saying: وَلَيَحْمِلُنَّ أَثْقالَهُمْ وَأَثْقالاً مَعَ أَثْقالِهِمْ (and they will surely carry their own burdens and other burdens along with their own burdens), this refers to those who are astray (الضّالّين) and those who lead others astray (المضلّين). For they carry the burdens of their misguidance (أثقال اضلالهم) along with the burdens of their being astray (أثقال ضلالهم), and all of that constitutes their own burdens (أوزارهم); there is nothing in it from the burdens of others. وَإِنْ تَدْعُ مُثْقَلَةٌ ** (and if a heavily laden soul calls [someone]), meaning a soul that has been weighed down by burdens (الأوزار), إِلى حِمْلِها ** (to carry part of its burden), meaning to carry some of its burdens, لا يُحْمَلْ مِنْهُ شَيْءٌ ** (nothing of it will be carried), meaning it will not be answered by carrying anything of it. He negated that its sin should be carried away from it, just as He negated that the sin of another should be carried upon it. وَلَوْ كانَ ذا قُرْبى (even if he be a relative), meaning even if the one called upon (المدعوّ) were a relative of hers. The one called upon (المدعوّ) is implied due to the indication of the phrase تَدْعُ (she calls) upon him. إِنَّما تُنْذِرُ الَّذِينَ يَخْشَوْنَ رَبَّهُمْ بِالْغَيْبِ وَأَقامُوا الصَّلاةَ ** (You can only warn those who fear their Lord in secret and maintain the prayer), for they are the ones who benefit (الْمُنْتَفِعُون) from the warning, and no others. وَمَنْ تَزَكَّى ** (And whoever purifies himself), meaning whoever purifies himself from the filth (دنس) of sins (المعاصي), فَإِنَّما يَتَزَكَّى لِنَفْسِهِ ** (purifies himself only for his own sake), since its benefit is for his soul, وَإِلَى اللهِ الْمَصِيرُ ** (and toward Allah is the ultimate return), so He will reward them for their purification (تزكيتهم).

footnote (١) **مَواخِرَ **: [Ships] running, cleaving the water.

(١٩) وَما يَسْتَوِي الْأَعْمى وَالْبَصِيرُ ** (19) The blind and the seeing are not equal, The disbeliever (الكافر) and the believer (المؤمن). (٢٠) وَلَا الظُّلُماتُ وَلَا النُّورُ ** (20) nor are the shadows and the light equal, Nor falsehood (الباطل) nor truth (الحقّ). (٢١) وَلَا الظِّلُّ وَلَا الْحَرُورُ ** (21) nor are the shade and the heat equal. Nor reward (الثواب) nor punishment (العقاب). The repetition of the negation of equality (نفي الاستواء) and its reiteration on both sides (على الشّقين) is for increased emphasis (لمزيد التأكيد). And الْحَرُورُ ** (scorching wind) is from heat (الحرّ); it predominates over the scorching winds (السموم). Al-Qummi (said): The shade (الظلّ) is the people (النّاس), and the scorching wind (الحرور) is the livestock (البهائم).

(٢٢) ** وَما يَسْتَوِي الْأَحْياءُ وَلَا الْأَمْواتُ ** (22) And the living and the dead are not equal This is another metaphor for the believers and the disbelievers, more eloquent than the first, and for that reason, the verb was repeated. And it was said [to refer] to the knowledgeable (العلماء) and the ignorant (الجهلاء). إِنَّ اللهَ يُسْمِعُ مَنْ يَشاءُ ** (Indeed Allah makes whomever He wishes hear) [meaning] His guidance, so He grants him success in understanding His signs and taking heed from His admonitions. وَما أَنْتَ بِمُسْمِعٍ مَنْ فِي الْقُبُورِ ** (and you cannot make those who are in the graves hear) [meaning] those who persist in disbelief.

(٢٣) إِنْ أَنْتَ إِلَّا نَذِيرٌ ** (23) You are only a warner. So, upon you is nothing except the warning (الإنذار), and as for listening (الاستماع), it is not upon you, nor do you have any means (حيلة) toward it regarding what is sealed upon their hearts.

(٢٤) إِنَّا أَرْسَلْناكَ بِالْحَقِّ بَشِيراً وَنَذِيراً وَإِنْ مِنْ أُمَّةٍ ** (24) Indeed, We have sent you with the truth as a bearer of good news and as a warner, and there is no nation (people of an era) except that there passed فِيها نَذِيرٌ ** (in it a warner) from a prophet (نبيّ) or the successor (وصيّ) of a prophet. Al-Qummi said: For every time, there is an Imam (إمام).

And in al-Kafi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): Muhammad did not die except that He had a commissioned warner (بعيث نذير). He said: If it is said, "No," then the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم would have neglected those of his community who were in the loins (أصلاب) of men. It was asked: "And is the Quran not sufficient for them?" He said: "Yes, if they find an interpreter (مفسّراً) for it." It was asked: "And did the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم not interpret it?" He said: "Yes, He interpreted it for one man, and He interpreted for the community the status (شأن) of that man, and he is Ali ibn Abi Talib (peace be upon him).

(٢٥) وَإِنْ يُكَذِّبُوكَ فَقَدْ كَذَّبَ الَّذِينَ مِنْ قَبْلِهِمْ جاءَتْهُمْ رُسُلُهُمْ بِالْبَيِّناتِ ** (25) If they deny you, those who were before them have already denied: their apostles brought them manifest proofs, with the miracles testifying to their prophethood وَبِالزُّبُرِ وَبِالْكِتابِ الْمُنِيرِ ** and the scriptures and the illuminating Book. such as the scriptures (صحف) of Abraham (peace be upon him), the Torah (التوراة), and the Gospel (الإنجيل).

(٢٦) ثُمَّ أَخَذْتُ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا فَكَيْفَ كانَ نَكِيرِ ** (26) Then I seized those who were faithless. So how was My censure? Meaning, My censure (إنكاري) through punishment (بالعقوبة). (٢٧) أَلَمْ تَرَ أَنَّ اللهَ أَنْزَلَ مِنَ السَّماءِ ماءً فَأَخْرَجْنا بِهِ ثَمَراتٍ مُخْتَلِفاً أَلْوانُها وَمِنَ الْجِبالِ جُدَدٌ ** (27) Have you not regarded that Allah sends down water from the sky, and with it We bring forth fruits of diverse hues? And in the mountains are streaks— Meaning, possessing streaks (ذو جدد), that is, paths (خطط) and ways (طرائق). بِيضٌ وَحُمْرٌ مُخْتَلِفٌ أَلْوانُها ** white and red, of diverse hues, in intensity (بالشدّة) and weakness (والضّعف). وَغَرابِيبُ سُودٌ (and intensely black). And among them are intensely black (غرابيب) ones of uniform color (متّحدة اللّون). And al-gharbīb (الغربيب) is an emphasis (تأكيد) for black (للأسود). Its right (وحقّه) is to follow the emphasized word (المؤكّد), but it was placed first (قدّم) for further emphasis (لمزيد التأكيد) due to the emphasis inherent in it, considering the implied (الإضمار) and the explicit (الاظهار).

(٢٨) ** وَمِنَ النَّاسِ وَالدَّوَابِّ وَالْأَنْعامِ مُخْتَلِفٌ أَلْوانُهُ كَذلِكَ ** (28) and of humans and beasts and cattle, there are likewise various hues. like the variation of the fruits and the mountains. إِنَّما يَخْشَى اللهَ مِنْ عِبادِهِ الْعُلَماءُ ** (28) Only those of His servants who have knowledge fear Allah. Indeed Allah is all-mighty, all-forgiving. since the prerequisite for fear (الخشية) is the knowledge (معرفة) of the One Who is feared (المخشيّ) and the knowledge (العلم) of His attributes and actions. Thus, whoever has more knowledge of Him is more fearful of Him. For this reason, the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله said: "I am the most fearful of you of Allah, and the most pious among you toward Him." إِنَّ اللهَ عَزِيزٌ غَفُورٌ (Indeed Allah is all-mighty, all-forgiving) is a justification (تعليل) for the necessity of fear (الخشية), because it indicates that He is the Punisher of the one who persists in his transgression (طغيانه), and the Forgiver of the one who repents from his disobedience (عصيانه).

In al-Majma', narrated from Imam al-Sadiq عليه السلام: "He means by the knowledgeable ones (العلماء) the one whose action confirms his saying, and whoever's action does not confirm his saying is not knowledgeable (عالم)." And in the Hadith: "The most knowledgeable among you concerning Allah is the one who is most fearful of Allah."

In al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Sajjad عليه السلام: "And the knowledge of Allah and the action are nothing but two familiar, harmonious things (ألفان مؤتلفان). So whoever knows Allah fears Him, and fear urges him to act in obedience to Allah. Indeed, the masters of knowledge (أرباب العلم) and their followers are those who knew Allah, so they acted for Him and desired Him. And Allah has said: إِنَّما يَخْشَى اللهَ مِنْ عِبادِهِ الْعُلَماءُ. (Only those of His servants who have knowledge fear Allah.)"

And narrated from Imam al-Sadiq عليه السلام: "Indeed, among worship (العبادة) is intense fear (شدّة الخوف) of Allah." Then He recited this Ayah.

And in Misbah al-Shari'ah, narrated from Him عليه السلام: "The evidence of fear (الخشية) is the magnification (التعظيم) of Allah, adherence to sincere obedience (خالص الطّاعة) and His commands, and fear (الخوف) and caution (الحذر). And the evidence for both of these is knowledge (العلم)." Then He recited this Ayah.

(٢٩)** إِنَّ الَّذِينَ يَتْلُونَ كِتابَ اللهِ وَأَقامُوا الصَّلاةَ وَأَنْفَقُوا مِمَّا رَزَقْناهُمْ سِرًّا وَعَلانِيَةً يَرْجُونَ تِجارَةً لَنْ تَبُورَ ** (29) Indeed, those who recite the Book of Allah and maintain the prayer and spend out of what We have provided them, secretly and openly, expect a commerce that will never perish. It will not become stagnant, nor will it perish through loss. And the commerce is the acquisition of the reward through obedience.

** لِيُوَفِّيَهُمْ أُجُورَهُمْ وَيَزِيدَهُمْ مِنْ فَضْلِهِ ** (That He may pay them their full rewards and enhance them out of His grace) commensurate with their deeds. In al-Majma', narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله: It is intercession (الشفاعة) for the one for whom the Fire has become incumbent, from among those to whom he showed kindness (معروفاً) in the world. إِنَّهُ غَفُورٌ ** for their slips (فرطاتهم) شَكُورٌ ** for their acts of obedience (طاعاتهم), meaning: He rewards them for them.

(٣١) وَالَّذِي أَوْحَيْنا إِلَيْكَ مِنَ الْكِتابِ ** (31) And what We have revealed to you of the Book meaning the Quran هُوَ الْحَقُّ مُصَدِّقاً لِما بَيْنَ يَدَيْهِ ** is the truth, confirming what was before it. of the heavenly Books ** إِنَّ اللهَ بِعِبادِهِ لَخَبِيرٌ بَصِيرٌ ** Indeed, Allah is well-aware of His servants, all-seeing. Knower of the inner (بالبواطن) and the outer (والظواهر).

(٣٢) ثُمَّ أَوْرَثْنَا الْكِتابَ الَّذِينَ اصْطَفَيْنا مِنْ عِبادِنا ** (32) Then We gave the Book as inheritance to those of Our servants whom We chose. This refers specifically to the Pure Progeny (العترة الطّاهرة). فَمِنْهُمْ ظالِمٌ لِنَفْسِهِ (Among them is one who wrongs his own soul) who does not know the Imam of his time, وَمِنْهُمْ مُقْتَصِدٌ (and among them is one who is moderate) who knows the Imam, وَمِنْهُمْ سابِقٌ بِالْخَيْراتِ بِإِذْنِ اللهِ** (and among them is one who is foremost in good deeds by Allah’s leave) who is the Imam.

In al-Basa'ir, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): It refers to the children of Ali and Fatimah (peace be upon them).

In al-Kafi, narrated from him (peace be upon him), he said: The بِالْخَيْراتِ ** (foremost in good deeds) is the Imam, the moderate (المقتصد) is the one who knows the Imam, and the one who wrongs his own soul (الظالم لنفسه) is the one who does not know the Imam.

And narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): It was said to him that it refers to the descendants of Fatimah (الفاطميّين). He said: "It is not as you suppose. The one who points his sword and calls people to misguidance does not enter into this." It was asked: "What is the one who wrongs his own soul (الظّالم لنفسه)?" He said: "The one who sits in his house and does not know the right (حقّ) of the Imam. The moderate (المقتصد) is the one who knows the right of the Imam, and the foremost in good deeds (السّابق بالخيرات) is the Imam."

And narrated from al-Kazim (peace be upon him): He recited this verse and said: "We are those whom Allah, the Almighty, the Majestic, chose and gave this Book as inheritance, in which there is a clarification of everything (تِبْيانُ كُلِّ شَيْءٍ)."

And narrated from al-Rida (peace be upon him): He was asked about it and said: "The children of Fatimah (peace be upon her). The foremost in good deeds (السابق بالخيرات) is the Imam, the moderate (المقتصد) is the one who knows the Imam, and the one who wrongs his own soul (الظالم لنفسه) is the one who does not know the Imam."

In al-Uyun, narrated from him (peace be upon him): Allah intended by this the Pure Progeny (العترة الطّاهرة). If He had intended the entire Ummah (nation), then all of them would be in Paradise, due to Allah's saying: فَمِنْهُمْ ظالِمٌ لِنَفْسِهِ ** [the rest of] the verse. Then He gathered all of them in Paradise when He said: جَنَّاتُ عَدْنٍ يَدْخُلُونَها [the rest of] the verse. Thus, the inheritance belongs to the Pure Progeny (العترة الطاهرة), not to others.

In al-Khara'ij, narrated from al-Zaki (peace be upon him): All of them are from the Household of Muhammad (may Allah bless him and his family and grant them peace). The one who wrongs his own soul (الظّالم لنفسه) is the one who does not acknowledge the Imam (peace be upon him), the moderate (المقتصد) is the one who knows the Imam, and the foremost in good deeds (السابق بالخيرات) is the Imam (peace be upon him).

And narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Because of Fatimah's (peace be upon her) greatness before Allah, Allah has forbidden her descendants to the Fire. And concerning them was revealed: ثُمَّ أَوْرَثْنَا الْكِتابَ ** [the rest of] the verse. Then he explained the three categories (الفرق الثّلاث) with what has preceded.

In al-Majma', narrated from him (peace be upon him): The one who wrongs his own soul (الظالم لنفسه) among us is the one who does not know the right (حقّ) of the Imam, and the moderate (المقتصد) among us is the one who knows the right of the Imam, and the foremost in good deeds (السابق بالخيرات) is the Imam. And all of these are forgiven.

In al-Ihtijaj, narrated from him (peace be upon him): He was asked about it, and it was said to him that it refers specifically to the children of Fatimah (peace be upon her). He said: "As for the one among the children of Fatimah (peace be upon her) who draws his sword and calls people to himself, to misguidance, he is not included in this verse." It was asked: "Who is included in it?" He said: "The one who wrongs his own soul (الظالم لنفسه) who does not call people to misguidance nor to guidance, and the moderate (المقتصد) among us, the People of the House (أهل البيت), who knows the right (حقّ) of the Imam, and the foremost in good deeds (السابق بالخيرات) is the Imam."

In al-Manaqib, narrated from him (peace be upon him): It was revealed concerning our right (حقّنا) and the right of our descendants (ذرّياتنا).

And in a narration from him, from his father (peace be upon them both): It is specific to us, and He meant us.

And narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): They are the Household of Muhammad (may the blessings of Allah be upon them).

In al-Ma'ani, narrated from him (peace be upon him): He was asked about it and said: "It was revealed concerning us, the People of the House (أهل البيت)." It was asked: "Who, then, is the one who wrongs his own soul (الظالم لنفسه)?" It was said: "The one among us, the People of the House, whose good deeds and evil deeds are equal, he is the one who wrongs his own soul." It was asked: "Who is the moderate (المقتصد) among you?" He said: "The one who worships Allah in both states until certainty (اليقين) comes to him." It was asked: "Who, then, is the foremost in good deeds (السّابق بالخيرات) among you?" He said: "The one who, by Allah, calls to the path of his Lord, enjoins the right (المعروف) and forbids the wrong (المنكر), and is neither a supporter of the misguiders nor an opponent of the betrayers, and does not accept the rule of the wicked (الفاسقين), except when he fears for his soul and his religion and finds no helpers."

And narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He was asked about it and said: "The one who wrongs his own soul (الظالم) circles around his own self, the moderate (المقتصد) circles around his heart, and the foremost (السابق) circles around his Lord, the Almighty, the Majestic."

In al-Majma', narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): As for the one who wrongs his own soul (الظالم لنفسه) among us, he is the one who performs one good deed and another evil deed. As for the moderate (المقتصد), he is the devoted, striving worshipper (المتعبّد المجتهد). And as for the foremost in good deeds (السابق بالخيرات), they are Ali, al-Hasan, and al-Husayn (peace be upon them), and whoever from the Household of Muhammad (may the blessings of Allah be upon them) was killed as a martyr.

In Sa'd al-Su'ud (1), narrated from him (peace be upon him): It is specific to us. As for the foremost in good deeds (السابق بالخيرات), they are Ali ibn Abi Talib, al-Hasan, al-Husayn (peace be upon them), and the martyr among us. As for the moderate (المقتصد), he is one who fasts during the day and stands in prayer during the night. As for the one who wrongs his own soul (الظالم لنفسه), he has what the people have, and he is forgiven.

ذلِكَ هُوَ الْفَضْلُ الْكَبِيرُ ** (32) That is the great grace. This is a reference to the inheritance (التوريث), or the choosing (الاصطفاء), or the being foremost (السّبق).

(٣٣) جَنَّاتُ عَدْنٍ يَدْخُلُونَها. (Gardens of Eden, which they will enter.) In al-Ma'ani, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He means the moderate (المقتصد) and the foremost (السابق). And in al-Majma', narrated from the Prophet (may Allah bless him and his progeny) regarding this verse, he said: As for the foremost (السابق), he will enter Paradise without reckoning. And as for the moderate (المقتصد), he will be reckoned with an easy reckoning. And as for the wrongdoer of his own soul (الظالم لنفسه), he will be detained in the station (المقام) then he will enter Paradise. They are those who قالُوا الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ الَّذِي أَذْهَبَ عَنَّا الْحَزَنَ (will say, 'All praise belongs to Allah who has removed grief from us') They will be adorned therein with bracelets of gold and pearls. And لؤلؤاً was read with the nasb (النّصب) case. وَلِباسُهُمْ فِيها حَرِيرٌ. (and their dress therein will be silk.)

(٣٤) ** وَقالُوا الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ الَّذِي أَذْهَبَ عَنَّا الْحَزَنَ إِنَّ رَبَّنا لَغَفُورٌ ** (And they will say, 'All praise belongs to Allah, who has removed grief from us. Indeed, our Lord is all-forgiving, all-thankful.') for the sinners, All-thankful (شَكُورٌ) for the obedient ones. (٣٥) ** الَّذِي أَحَلَّنا دارَ الْمُقامَةِ ** (Him who has settled us in the everlasting abode) the abode of permanence (دار الإقامة) ** مِنْ فَضْلِهِ ** (by His grace) from His bestowal (انعامه) and His favor (تفضّله). ** لا يَمَسُّنا فِيها نَصَبٌ ** (No toil befalls us therein) toil (تعب) وَلا يَمَسُّنا فِيها لُغُوبٌ (nor does any fatigue befall us therein) weariness (كلال), since there is no obligation (تكليف) therein, nor any exertion (كدّ). The negation of toil (النّصب) was followed by the negation of what follows it, for emphasis (مبالغة). Al-Qummi said: Al-Nasab (النّصب) is hardship (العناء), and al-Lughub (اللّغوب) is laziness (الكسل) and distress (الضّجر). And ** دارَ الْمُقامَةِ ** is the abode of permanence (دار البقاء).

In al-Kafi and al-Qummi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him), he said: The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله said: "When the believer enters his dwellings in Paradise (الجنّة), a crown of sovereignty (الملك) and honor (الكرامة) is placed upon his head, and he is dressed in robes (حلل) of gold, silver, pearls (الدرّ), and rubies (الياقوت), strung together in a wreath (الإكليل) beneath the crown. He is dressed in seventy silk robes (حلّة) of various colors, woven with gold, silver, pearls (اللّؤلؤ), and red rubies (الياقوت الأحمر). That is His saying, the Almighty: ** يُحَلَّوْنَ فِيها مِنْ أَساوِرَ ** (They will be adorned therein with bracelets) the Ayah." He said: "Then his wife, the Hur al-'Ayn (الحوراء), emerges toward him from her tent, walking forward, surrounded by her attendants (وصفاؤها). She is wearing seventy robes woven with rubies, pearls, and chrysolite (الزّبرجد), dyed with musk (المسك) and ambergris (عنبر). Upon her head is the crown of honor (تاج الكرامة), and on her feet are two sandals of gold, studded with rubies and pearls, their straps being red ruby. When she approaches the friend of Allah (وليّ الله) and he intends to stand up for her out of longing (شوقاً), she says to him: 'O friend of Allah, this is not a day of toil (تعب) or hardship (نصب), so do not stand. I am for you, and you are for me.' "Then he embraces her for a period of five hundred years of the years of this world (الدنيا). He does not tire of her, nor does she tire of him." He said: "Then he looks at her neck, and behold, upon it is a necklace made of red ruby stalks (قصب ياقوت أحمر), in the middle of which is a tablet inscribed: 'You, O friend of Allah, are My beloved (حبيبي), and I, the Hur al-'Ayn (الحوراء), am your beloved (حبيبتك). My soul reached its limit for you, and your soul reached its limit for Me.' "Then Allah sends to him a thousand angels who congratulate him on Paradise (الجنّة) and marry him to the Hur al-'Ayn (الحوراء)." The Hadith (الحديث)—its entirety has already passed in Surat al-Ra'd (سورة الرَّعد).

In Sa'd al-Su'ud (1), narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله, in a Hadith wherein He mentions what Allah has prepared for the lovers of Ali (peace be upon him) on the Day of Resurrection (يوم القيامة). He said: "When they enter their dwellings, they find the angels congratulating them on the honor (كرامة) bestowed by their Lord. Until they are settled in their resting place (قرارهم), it will be said to them: 'Have you found what your Lord promised to be true?' They will say: 'Yes, our Lord. We are pleased, so be pleased with us.' He will say: 'By My pleasure with you and by your love for the Household (أهل بيت) of My Prophet, you have settled in My abode and shaken hands with the angels. Congratulations, congratulations! A gift that is unending (غير مجذوذ), in which there is no disturbance (تنغيص).' It is then that they say: ** الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ الَّذِي أَذْهَبَ عَنَّا الْحَزَنَ ** (All praise belongs to Allah, who has removed grief from us) the Ayah.

footnote (١) From the works of the ascetic (*الزاهد*), the Master (*السيّد*), the Beauty of the Gnostics (*جمال العارفين*), Abu al-Qasim Ali ibn Musa al-Tawus al-Husayni.

(٣٦) وَالَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا لَهُمْ نارُ جَهَنَّمَ لا يُقْضى عَلَيْهِمْ ** (36) But as for those who disbelieve, there is for them the Fire of hell. They will not be done away with, meaning, a second death will not be decreed upon them so that they might die and find rest وَلا يُخَفَّفُ عَنْهُمْ مِنْ عَذابِها ** nor will the punishment of it be lightened for them. Rather, whenever it subsides, they will be increased in blazing fire. كَذلِكَ نَجْزِي كُلَّ كَفُورٍ Thus do We requite every ingrate. And it was recited (يُجْزَى) based on the passive construction (بناء المفعول).

(٣٧) وَهُمْ يَصْطَرِخُونَ فِيها ** (37) And they will cry out therein: 'Our Lord! Bring us out, that we may act righteously, differently from what we used to do.' They seek aid with screaming/crying out. ** رَبَّنا أَخْرِجْنا نَعْمَلْ صالِحاً غَيْرَ الَّذِي كُنَّا نَعْمَلُ ** by implying the saying. ** أَوَلَمْ نُعَمِّرْكُمْ ما يَتَذَكَّرُ فِيهِ مَنْ تَذَكَّرَ وَجاءَكُمُ النَّذِيرُ ** is a response from Allah and a rebuke to them. And ** ما يَتَذَكَّرُ فِيهِ ** encompasses every lifespan in which remembrance (التذكّر) is possible.

In al-Faqih, al-Khisal, and al-Majma', narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): "And this is a rebuke for the son of eighteen years." And in Nahj al-Balaghah: "The lifespan in which Allah grants an excuse (أعذر) to the son of Adam is sixty years."

And in al-Majma', narrated marfu'an from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: "Whoever Allah grants a lifespan of sixty years, He has granted him an excuse (أعذر عليه)." ** فَذُوقُوا فَما لِلظَّالِمِينَ مِنْ نَصِيرٍ ** who repels the punishment from them.

(٣٨) إِنَّ اللهَ عالِمُ غَيْبِ السَّماواتِ وَالْأَرْضِ ** (38) Indeed Allah is the knower of the unseen of the heavens and the earth. Nothing hidden is concealed from Him, so their conditions are not concealed from Him. إِنَّهُ عَلِيمٌ بِذاتِ الصُّدُورِ. Indeed He is aware of what is in the breasts. (٣٩) هُوَ الَّذِي جَعَلَكُمْ خَلائِفَ فِي الْأَرْضِ ** (39) It is He who made you successors on the earth. Al-Qummi (said): To you belong the keys (مقاليد) of disposal therein, or He made you successors (خلفاً) generation after generation (بعد خلف). فَمَنْ كَفَرَ فَعَلَيْهِ كُفْرُهُ ** So whoever disbelieves, his disbelief shall be against him. (Meaning) the recompense (جزاء) of his disbelief. وَلا يَزِيدُ الْكافِرِينَ كُفْرُهُمْ عِنْدَ رَبِّهِمْ إِلَّا مَقْتاً وَلا يَزِيدُ الْكافِرِينَ كُفْرُهُمْ إِلَّا خَساراً ** And the disbelievers’ disbelief increases them only in odium in the sight of their Lord, and the disbelievers’ disbelief increases them only in loss. This is an explanation for it. The repetition is to indicate that the requirement of disbelief for each of the two matters (odium and loss) is independent in requiring its ugliness (قبحه) and the necessity of avoiding it (وجوب التجنّب عنه). What is meant by al-maqt (المقت), which is the severest hatred (أشدّ البغض), is the hatred (مقت) of Allah, and al-khusar (الخسار) is the loss (خسار) of the Hereafter (الآخرة).

The Almighty's saying: (٤٠)** قُلْ أَرَأَيْتُمْ شُرَكاءَكُمُ ** (40) Say, 'Tell me about your partners Inform Me about these partners.

الَّذِينَ تَدْعُونَ مِنْ دُونِ اللهِ ** whom you invoke besides Allah: meaning their deities (آلهتهم). The attribution (الإضافة) is to them because they made them partners (شركاء) to Allah, or [partners] to themselves in what they possess.

أَرُونِي ما ذا خَلَقُوا مِنَ الْأَرْضِ ** show me what they have created of the earth, [This phrase is] a substitute (بدل) for أَرَأَيْتُمْ (Tell me about).

أَمْ لَهُمْ شِرْكٌ فِي السَّماواتِ ** or do they have a share in the heavens? [Meaning] a partnership (شركة) with Allah in their creation, whereby they deserved a partnership in the essential Divinity (الألوهيّة ذاتية).

أَمْ آتَيْناهُمْ ** Did We give them meaning the partners (الشّركاء) or the polytheists (المشركين).

كِتاباً ** a Book, which speaks to the fact that We have taken partners (شركاء).

فَهُمْ عَلى بَيِّنَةٍ مِنْهُ ** so that they stand on a manifest proof from it? [Meaning] upon a proof (حجّة) from that Book that they possess an appointed partnership (شركة جعليّة).

And it was read (وقرئ) as على بيّنات (upon manifest proofs), indicating that in such a matter, the mutual support (تعاضد) of evidences (الدّلائل) is indispensable.

بَلْ إِنْ يَعِدُ الظَّالِمُونَ بَعْضُهُمْ بَعْضاً إِلَّا غُرُوراً ** Rather the wrongdoers promise one another nothing but delusion. [This delusion is] that they (بأنّهم) are their intercessors (شفعاؤهم) with Allah, interceding for them through drawing near to them.

** إِنَّ اللهَ يُمْسِكُ السَّماواتِ وَالْأَرْضَ أَنْ تَزُولا وَلَئِنْ زالَتا إِنْ أَمْسَكَهُما مِنْ أَحَدٍ مِنْ بَعْدِهِ ** (41) Indeed Allah sustains the heavens and the earth lest they should fall apart, and if they were to fall apart, none would sustain them after Him. after Allah, or after the falling apart (الزّوال). إِنَّهُ كانَ حَلِيماً غَفُوراً ** (41) Indeed He is all-clement, all-forgiving. since He sustains them, even though they were worthy of being utterly destroyed, as the Mighty and Majestic said: تَكادُ السَّماواتُ يَتَفَطَّرْنَ مِنْهُ وَتَنْشَقُّ الْأَرْضُ.** (The heavens are about to be rent apart because of it, and the earth to split open.)

In al-Kafi, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): He was asked whether Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, carries the Throne (العرش) or the Throne carries Him. He (peace be upon him) said: "Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, is the Bearer of the Throne (العرش), the heavens, the earth, what is in them, and what is between them. That is the saying of Allah, the Almighty: **إِنَّ اللهَ يُمْسِكُ السَّماواتِ وَالْأَرْضَ أَنْ تَزُولا ** (Indeed Allah sustains the heavens and the earth lest they should fall apart) up to the end of the Ayah."

And in al-Ikmal, narrated from al-Rida (peace be upon him) in a tradition: "Through us, Allah sustains السَّماواتِ وَالْأَرْضَ أَنْ تَزُولا (the heavens and the earth lest they should fall apart)." And narrated from them (peace be upon them): "Were it not for those of us (مِنَّا) who are on the earth, it would sink with its inhabitants."

(٤٢) وَأَقْسَمُوا بِاللهِ جَهْدَ أَيْمانِهِمْ لَئِنْ جاءَهُمْ نَذِيرٌ لَيَكُونُنَّ أَهْدى مِنْ إِحْدَى الْأُمَمِ ** (42) They swore by Allah with their most solemn oaths that if a warner came to them, they would be better guided than any of the nations. It was said that this was because when Quraysh learned that the People of the Book (أهل الكتاب) had denied their messenger, they said: "May Allah curse the Jews and the Christians! If a messenger had come to us, لَيَكُونُنَّ أَهْدى مِنْ إِحْدَى الْأُمَمِ ** (they would be better guided than any of the nations)." A narrative concerning this meaning will come in Surat Sad, if Allah wills. فَلَمَّا جاءَهُمْ نَذِيرٌ ** (But when a warner came to them) meaning Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله ما زادَهُمْ ** (it only increased them) meaning the warner (النذير) or his coming إِلَّا نُفُوراً ** (in aversion), meaning distance from the truth (تباعداً عن الحقّ).

(٤٣) اسْتِكْباراً فِي الْأَرْضِ وَمَكْرَ السَّيِّئِ وَلا يَحِيقُ (out of arrogance in the land and devising evil schemes. But evil schemes rebound only on their authors.) And it does not encompass (يُحِيطُ) الْمَكْرُ السَّيِّئُ إِلَّا بِأَهْلِهِ ** And it is the schemer (الْمَاكِر). It was said: And it had encompassed them on the Day of Badr. فَهَلْ يَنْظُرُونَ ** (Do they await) They await (يَنْتَظِرُونَ) إِلَّا سُنَّتَ الْأَوَّلِينَ ** (anything but the precedent of the former peoples?) The way (سُنَّة) of Allah concerning them, by punishing those who denied His messengers. فَلَنْ تَجِدَ لِسُنَّتِ اللهِ تَبْدِيلاً وَلَنْ تَجِدَ لِسُنَّتِ اللهِ تَحْوِيلاً** (You will never find any change in Allah’s precedent, and you will never find any shift in Allah’s precedent.) Since He does not change it by making the punishment other than it, nor does He shift it by transferring it to others besides them.

(٤٤) أَوَلَمْ يَسِيرُوا فِي الْأَرْضِ فَيَنْظُرُوا كَيْفَ كانَ عاقِبَةُ الَّذِينَ مِنْ قَبْلِهِمْ ** (44) Have they not traveled in the land and seen how was the fate of those who were before them? It was said that this is evidence against them based on what they observe in their journeys to al-Sham (الشام), al-Yaman (اليمن), and al-Iraq (العراق) from the traces of those who passed away. And al-Qummi (القمّيّ) said: Have they not looked in the Quran and in the accounts of the perished nations? وَكانُوا أَشَدَّ مِنْهُمْ قُوَّةً وَما كانَ اللهُ لِيُعْجِزَهُ مِنْ شَيْءٍ and they were mightier than them in power. Nothing in the heavens or in the earth can frustrate Allah. so that it precedes Him and escapes Him. فِي السَّماواتِ وَلا فِي الْأَرْضِ إِنَّهُ كانَ عَلِيماً in the heavens or in the earth. Indeed, He is all-knowing, of all things, قَدِيراً **all-powerful. over them.

(٤٥) The Almighty's saying: ** وَلَوْ يُؤاخِذُ اللهُ النَّاسَ بِما كَسَبُوا ** of sins ما تَرَكَ عَلى ظَهْرِها ** the surface of the earth مِنْ دَابَّةٍ ** that creeps upon it, due to the ill-omen of their sins وَلكِنْ يُؤَخِّرُهُمْ إِلى أَجَلٍ مُسَمًّى فَإِذا جاءَ أَجَلُهُمْ فَإِنَّ اللهَ كانَ بِعِبادِهِ بَصِيراً ** (45) Had Allah taken mankind to task for what they have earned, He would not have left any creature on its surface. But He reprieves them until a specified term. Then, when their term comes, Allah is indeed watchful of His servants. so He will recompense them for their deeds. The reward for seeing its interpretation (ثواب راءتها) has already preceded in the end of Surah Saba'.
